title: centaurea × romanica
intro: Centaurea × romanica, also known as Roman cornflower, is a hybrid plant species within the family Asteraceae. It is native to Europe, Asia, and parts of Africa, and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant's leaves and flowers have been used to treat various health issues, such as fever, digestive problems, and respiratory infections, offering health benefits like reduced inflammation and improved immune function. In herbal preparations, Centaurea × romanica is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to take advantage of its medicinal properties, making it a valuable addition to natural remedies.
